issues.title: Unspecified behavior when deleting a project that has tasks
issues.description: The specification requires create/rename/delete projects and that tasks may be assigned to a single project, but it does not define what should happen to tasks when their parent project is deleted. This is a major data-loss / UX ambiguity (delete tasks, cascade, or orphan tasks?).
issues.recommendation: Define explicit behavior for project deletion: either (a) cascade-delete tasks with explicit confirmation and an undo window, (b) unassign tasks (set projectId to null) and optionally move to an 'Inbox' or 'Unassigned' view, or (c) offer a user-facing choice. Add acceptance criteria, UI flows, and tests for each option, including confirmations and undo behavior.

issues.title: No concurrency / multi-tab synchronization strategy
issues.description: Spec assumes local-only storage but does not address concurrent usage (multiple tabs/windows). Without handling, two tabs can diverge, cause lost updates, or show stale data. IndexedDB changes in one tab are not automatically propagated to React state unless explicitly handled.
issues.recommendation: Specify a lightweight synchronization approach for same-browser multi-tab scenarios: use BroadcastChannel or storage events to notify other tabs of changes, include an events contract (payloads describing mutation type and entity IDs), and define how optimistic updates / conflicts are resolved. Add tests that simulate multi-tab mutations and ensure UI consistency.

issues.title: Optimistic UI updates lack rollback and error handling requirements
issues.description: The spec mandates optimistic UI updates for snappy UX but does not define failure modes, rollback semantics, or user feedback when storage operations fail (e.g., quota exceeded, IndexedDB errors). Without this, users may see changes that were never persisted.
issues.recommendation: Define error states and rollback semantics: how the UI should revert on write failure, notification messages, retry UX, and telemetry/logging. Add acceptance criteria and tests for failure scenarios (e.g., simulated IndexedDB write errors, quota exceeded).

issues.title: Missing schema migration/versioning and migration testing
issues.description: The specification sets DB name and version but does not define migration strategy for future schema changes (new fields, object store changes) or how to handle upgrades and backward compatibility. No tests for migrations are specified.
issues.recommendation: Specify DB migration patterns (onupgradeneeded handlers, transforming existing records, versioned migration steps). Add acceptance criteria and unit/integration tests that run migrations from prior versions to the current schema and verify data integrity.

issues.title: Undefined behavior for 'position' field and manual ordering
issues.description: position is included for manual ordering but no rules are provided for how position is calculated, re-indexed, or handled for insertions/deletions. Sorting by position, gaps, collisions, and rebalancing strategies are unspecified.
issues.recommendation: Define ordering semantics: whether position is a floating-point/decimal to ease insertions, how to handle reordering UI events, when to recompute positions, and how to persist order across projects. Add E2E tests for reordering, insertion at arbitrary positions, and large reorders.

issues.title: No explicit behavior for tasks assigned to non-existent projects
issues.description: There is no stated validation when a task references a projectId that does not exist (e.g., stale ID after project deletion or import). This can lead to orphaned references and UI confusion.
issues.recommendation: Define validation rules: either prevent saving a task with invalid projectId, auto-nullify projectId and surface to user, or reject operations with a clear error. Add unit tests for creating/updating tasks with invalid projectId and for listing tasks where the project is missing.

issues.title: Storage fallback and quota behavior not fully specified
issues.description: The spec allows fallback from IndexedDB to localStorage but lacks details: when to fall back, how to detect IndexedDB availability or failure, how to handle storage quota exceeded errors, and migration between storages.
issues.recommendation: Document exact fallback criteria, migration steps between IndexedDB and localStorage, and user-visible messaging when storage is full or unavailable. Add tests simulating fallback and quota errors.

issues.title: Date/time handling, formats, and timezone semantics are unspecified
issues.description: dueDate and timestamps are described as ISO timestamps, but there is no guidance on timezone handling, parsing/display formatting, or user expectations for dueDate (date-only vs date-time). This can cause confusion across locales and when computing due date sorting.
issues.recommendation: Standardize date handling: decide on UTC storage (ISO 8601 with timezone) vs local-time storage, define display formatting per user's locale, and acceptance criteria for dueDate granularity. Add tests for sorting by dueDate across timezone scenarios and for correctly displaying/round-tripping dates.

issues.title: Lack of explicit UX for deletion confirmations and undo
issues.description: Deleting tasks and projects is allowed, but the spec does not require confirmation dialogs, toast-undo affordances, or recovery options. This increases risk of accidental data loss.
issues.recommendation: Specify when to show confirmations (project deletion should require confirmation), and implement a short undo window (e.g., toast with 'Undo' for X seconds) for task and project deletions. Add acceptance criteria and E2E tests for delete + undo flows.

issues.title: Acceptance criteria missing for edge cases and non-core flows
issues.description: Acceptance Criteria covers main flows but omits many edge cases: large dataset performance (5k tasks), behavior when storage is corrupted or cleared, multi-tab state, reordering correctness, and fallback behavior for non-supported browsers.
issues.recommendation: Extend acceptance criteria to include these edge cases (e.g., app remains responsive with 5k tasks; recover gracefully from storage corruption by offering import/repair). Add corresponding unit/E2E tests and CI checks for these scenarios.

issues.title: Testing strategy lacks explicit coverage targets and E2E matrix
issues.description: The spec suggests 60–70% coverage for core modules but does not define which modules are in-scope for coverage, nor which browsers/devices or test environments the E2E suite should run on (desktop/tablet/phone breakpoints).
issues.recommendation: Set clear coverage targets per layer (services >= 80%, components >= 70%) and an E2E matrix (Chrome desktop + Firefox + Safari on macOS, and at least one mobile viewport). Include smoke tests for the deployed artifact in CI prior to release.

issues.title: Accessibility requirements are vague and incomplete
issues.description: Spec asks for 'basic WCAG AA-ish patterns' and keyboard focus on primary controls, but does not enumerate keyboard interactions, focus order, ARIA roles, or color contrast requirements. Automated or manual accessibility checks are only lightly mentioned.
issues.recommendation: Define concrete accessibility requirements: keyboard shortcuts/behaviors (add task, toggle complete, delete confirmation), required ARIA roles for task list and dialogs, minimum color contrast ratio, and include axe or similar tests in CI and accessibility E2E checks.

issues.title: Search/filter semantics and edge behaviors not fully specified
issues.description: listTasks supports an optional search parameter but the spec doesn't define matching rules (case sensitivity, fuzzy, which fields are searched), nor pagination or result limits when search matches many items.
issues.recommendation: Specify search semantics (case-insensitive substring on title and description), introduce optional pagination or virtualized rendering for large result sets, and add tests for search behavior and performance under large datasets.

issues.title: No import/export/backup facility or data portability guidance
issues.description: Given local-only storage, users may lose data if they clear browser storage or switch devices. The spec does not provide an export/import or backup mechanism.
issues.recommendation: Add an optional JSON export/import API and UI to allow users to backup and restore data. Define acceptance criteria and tests for export/import correctness and version compatibility.

issues.title: UI deep-linking and routing behaviors are underspecified
issues.description: Routing is listed as optional but the spec doesn't define behavior for deep links to a project or task, or what happens on page load with URL params (e.g., ?projectId=...).
issues.recommendation: Decide whether to include client-side routes for direct links to projects/tasks. If supported, specify URL schema and add tests for deep-link navigation and proper state hydration from storage.
